# CURRICULUM
# Generated by: Session 1 - May 25 2026
# Course: CMU 15-213
# Lab: L0 C Programming Lab
# Total projects planned: 30
# Last updated: 2026-05-25

# PHASE 1
# Goal: Own memory pointers and structs at the byte level
# Prerequisites: None. Starting from zero.

PROJECT 1
  Name     : Bytes and Variables
  Concept  : A variable is a named region of bytes at a real address in RAM
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student thinks in variable names not addresses
  Builds on: Nothing
  Unlocks  : Everything. No mental model works without this.

PROJECT 2
  Name     : Addresses and printf
  Concept  : Every variable has an address you can read with &
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student does not know variables live at real locations
  Builds on: Project 1
  Unlocks  : Project 3

PROJECT 3
  Name     : What a Pointer Actually Is
  Concept  : A pointer is an integer that holds an address
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student treats pointers as magical not as integers
  Builds on: Project 2
  Unlocks  : Project 4

PROJECT 4
  Name     : Dereferencing
  Concept  : The * operator follows the address and touches the bytes
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Confusing pointer value with value at pointed address
  Builds on: Project 3
  Unlocks  : Project 5

PROJECT 5
  Name     : Pointer Assignment vs Value Assignment
  Concept  : p = q moves the address. *p = *q moves the value
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student conflates changing pointer with changing data
  Builds on: Project 4
  Unlocks  : Project 6

PROJECT 6
  Name     : Structs as Byte Blocks
  Concept  : A struct is a contiguous region of bytes with named offsets
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student thinks struct fields are separate variables
  Builds on: Project 5
  Unlocks  : Project 7

PROJECT 7
  Name     : Pointers to Structs and the Arrow Operator
  Concept  : p->field is exactly (*p).field nothing more
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Arrow operator feels magical instead of mechanical
  Builds on: Project 6
  Unlocks  : Project 8

PROJECT 8
  Name     : Strings in C
  Concept  : A string is not a type it is a convention about bytes
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student treats strings as values not as byte arrays
  Builds on: Project 7
  Unlocks  : Project 9

PROJECT 9
  Name     : strlen and the Null Terminator
  Concept  : strlen counts until it hits zero not including zero
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Off-by-one on string allocation
  Builds on: Project 8
  Unlocks  : Project 10

PROJECT 10
  Name     : malloc
  Concept  : malloc returns a pointer to n bytes or NULL on failure
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student does not check NULL return from malloc
  Builds on: Project 9
  Unlocks  : Project 11

PROJECT 11
  Name     : free
  Concept  : free marks bytes as available it does not zero them
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student thinks free erases memory
  Builds on: Project 10
  Unlocks  : Project 12

PROJECT 12
  Name     : malloc and free Must Balance
  Concept  : Every malloc needs exactly one free no more no less
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Leaks and double-frees both come from imbalance
  Builds on: Project 11
  Unlocks  : Project 13

PROJECT 13
  Name     : NULL Pointer
  Concept  : NULL is address zero dereferencing it is always wrong
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student does not guard against NULL before dereferencing
  Builds on: Project 12
  Unlocks  : Project 14

PROJECT 14
  Name     : Copying Strings with malloc and strcpy
  Concept  : To own a string you must allocate space and copy bytes
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student stores pointer to caller string instead of copy
  Builds on: Project 13
  Unlocks  : Project 15

PROJECT 15
  Name     : Typedef and Struct Aliases
  Concept  : typedef gives a struct a usable name no new behavior
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student confused by list_ele_t vs struct list_ele
  Builds on: Project 14
  Unlocks  : Project 16

PROJECT 16
  Name     : Linked List One Node by Hand
  Concept  : A node is a struct with a pointer to the next node
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student cannot build the physical picture of a linked node
  Builds on: Project 15
  Unlocks  : Project 17

PROJECT 17
  Name     : Linking Two Nodes
  Concept  : Setting next pointer connects two heap-allocated nodes
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student does not see that next is just an address in a field
  Builds on: Project 16
  Unlocks  : Project 18

PROJECT 18
  Name     : Traversing a Linked List
  Concept  : A loop follows next pointers until NULL never recurse
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student writes recursive traversal stack overflow at 1M nodes
  Builds on: Project 17
  Unlocks  : Project 19

PROJECT 19
  Name     : sizeof
  Concept  : sizeof gives compile-time byte count not runtime length
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : sizeof pointer vs sizeof pointee confusion
  Builds on: Project 18
  Unlocks  : Project 20

PROJECT 20
  Name     : The malloc sizeof Pattern
  Concept  : Allocating a struct always uses sizeof that struct
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 1
  Gap      : Student hardcodes sizes instead of using sizeof
  Builds on: Project 19
  Unlocks  : Phase 2

# PHASE 2
# Goal: Implement all seven queue functions correctly
# Prerequisites: Phase 1 complete

PROJECT 21
  Name     : queue_new
  Concept  : Creating a queue means allocating a queue_t and zeroing fields
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 2
  Gap      : Student does not initialize fields after malloc
  Builds on: Project 20
  Unlocks  : Project 22

PROJECT 22
  Name     : queue_free
  Concept  : Free nodes before freeing the queue struct itself
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 2
  Gap      : Student frees queue_t first loses pointer to nodes
  Builds on: Project 21
  Unlocks  : Project 23

PROJECT 23
  Name     : queue_insert_head
  Concept  : Allocate node copy string point node to old head update head
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 2
  Gap      : Student updates head before saving old head list lost
  Builds on: Project 22
  Unlocks  : Project 24

PROJECT 24
  Name     : queue_insert_tail
  Concept  : tail pointer makes append O(1) update tail on every insert
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 2
  Gap      : Student walks list to find tail O(n) fails at 1M elements
  Builds on: Project 23
  Unlocks  : Project 25

PROJECT 25
  Name     : queue_size
  Concept  : Return the stored size field never traverse the list
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 2
  Gap      : Student counts nodes O(n) fails performance test
  Builds on: Project 24
  Unlocks  : Project 26

PROJECT 26
  Name     : queue_remove_head
  Concept  : Save next copy string out free node update head
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 2
  Gap      : Student frees node before copying string out of it
  Builds on: Project 25
  Unlocks  : Project 27

PROJECT 27
  Name     : queue_reverse
  Concept  : Walk list once reverse each next pointer swap head and tail
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 2
  Gap      : Student allocates new nodes violates lab constraint
  Builds on: Project 26
  Unlocks  : Project 28

PROJECT 28
  Name     : Edge Cases NULL Queue and Empty Queue
  Concept  : NULL queue and empty queue are different and both must work
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab
  Course   : CMU 15-213
  Phase    : 2
  Gap      : Student handles one but not the other crashes on traces
  Builds on: Project 27
  Unlocks  : Project 29

PROJECT 29

  Concept  : ASan reports tell you exact file line and violation type
  Lab      : L0 C Programming Lab

  Phase    : 2

  Concept  : driver.py is the ground truth all 100 points
